snippets.title: Import stable logic instead of pasting it
snippets.code:
# prompts.py (a module, tested separately)
# def build_prompt(question: str) -> str: ...

# notebook cell
%load_ext autoreload
%autoreload 2
from prompts import build_prompt   # reuse, don't copy-paste

build_prompt("What is RAG?")
snippets.explanation: Reusable logic lives in a module; the notebook imports it and stays focused on narrative.

snippets.title: Keep secrets out of the file
snippets.code:
import os
api_key = os.environ["OPENAI_API_KEY"]   # not hard-coded
# In Colab: from google.colab import userdata; userdata.get('OPENAI_API_KEY')
snippets.explanation: Reading from the environment prevents keys from being saved into the shared notebook.

snippets.title: Strip outputs for version control
snippets.code:
pip install nbstripout
nbstripout --install            # auto-strip outputs on commit (git filter)

# optional: pair a .py for clean diffs
pip install jupytext
jupytext --set-formats ipynb,py:percent notebook.ipynb
snippets.explanation: nbstripout removes noisy outputs; jupytext keeps a reviewable .py twin.
